description: Alerting rules for the Azure Security Finding Chain scenario (Defender, Sentinel, Activity Log)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Security Finding Chain — Defender Alert
rules.investigationGuide:
# Defender HIGH/CRITICAL Alerts

The threat detector raised a HIGH or CRITICAL finding.

## Triage
1. List findings: `FROM logs-azure.defender-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND azure.defender.severity IN ("High", "Critical") | KEEP @timestamp, azure.defender.severity, azure.defender.alert_type, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Identify the affected resource (resource ID or Entra principal)
3. If Exfiltration → see Data Exfil runbook; if PrivEsc → see Entra ID / RBAC runbook

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call** for HIGH/CRITICAL
- Customer data or secrets implicated

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Security Finding Chain — Multi-Stage Chain Burst
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multi-Stage Chain Burst

Multiple alert-class docs across detectors in a short window.

## Triage
1. Detector breakdown: `FROM logs-azure.defender-*, logs-azure.sentinel-*, logs-azure.activitylogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30m AND event.kind == "alert" | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY event.dataset | SORT findings DESC`
2. One detector spiking = less interesting; three = investigate
3. Check for shared source.ip or user.name

## Escalate when
- Spike crosses three or more detectors with shared pivot
- Includes HIGH/CRITICAL severity findings

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Security Finding Chain — Sentinel Incident Created
rules.investigationGuide:
# Sentinel Incident Created

A Sentinel incident aggregates correlated detections — review entities and tactics.

## Triage
1. Recent incidents: `FROM logs-azure.sentinel-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.kind == "alert" | STATS incidents = COUNT(*) BY user.name | SORT incidents DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. One noisy analytic rule vs. many principals — breadth and new entities are higher signal
3. Cross-check Defender alerts, `logs-azure.defender_recommendations-*` posture (e.g. `azure.defender_recommendations.status`), and Activity Logs in the same window

## Escalate when
- Incident touches regulated workloads (PCI, HIPAA, SOC 2)
- Same resource or principal is unhealthy across Defender for Cloud recommendations and Sentinel

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Security Finding Chain — Defender High Severity
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multiple Defender Alerts With Source IP

Burst of findings with external source IPs — likely external scanner or attacker.

## Triage
1. Top IPs: `FROM logs-azure.defender-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.kind == "alert" AND source.ip IS NOT NULL | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY source.ip | SORT findings DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. One IP = targeted; many IPs = broader campaign
3. Check threat intel feeds for the IP

## Escalate when
- Source IP on known threat feed
- Targets include customer-facing resources
